widgets_values: First we adjust the scale to 1k . Marigold operates best at this or 768 resolution . Lotus slows down significantly after 1k
This subgroup uses Marigold to Extract the passes of the material

We are using Lotus and it is trained on camera space, not world space. This means for orthgraphic world space textures, we can see a gradient shift in our normal and height maps . We can augment this by padding the image first . Ten graphhing back down . Padd percentage determines how much buffer you want . THe bigger the percentage , teh flatter the maps .

We are generating two normals that you can choose or belnd together in the normal map processory. Lotus G is generative and can give more detailed results , but lotus D is much more predicatable . Depending n your pipeline , you make choose to omit one of these processors.
Westore the size of the original image, to translate back into
Texture should be orthgraphic , if it has visible seams , you can fix it in the end wit hteh Seam Patcher Loop

groups.title: Extract Depth , Normal and Ambient Occlusion

nodes.title: Understanding Gamma and Marigold
widgets_values:
textures that are saved Save are stored in a gamma-corrected (non-linear) space—commonly using a gamma of 2.2. 

Marigold Lighting model assumes Linear color space and produces maps in linear . Applying a correction of ~ 0.45 will correct for darkened images, and effectively linearize the output. 

Marigold appearance model ( rougness and metallic) Assumes SRGB Space , we need to account for this by adjusting the gamma to 2.2

Gamma Values can be changed but not necessary to adjust for Remix purposes.
